text: fat is 29g. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
carbs is 4.1g. sugar, glycemic index, and starch are doing most of the work. for dairy, the question is whether the payoff is worth the baggage.
protein is 38g. protein quality is a real strength here. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
vitamin b12 at 44% DV, vitamin a at 26% DV stand out most. that is only mild support overall.
calcium at 92% DV, zinc at 29% DV, magnesium at 11% DV stand out most. that is major mineral support.

the upsides first. easy protein delivery for normal diets because dairy gets real credit for convenience. clear calcium identity because that is a core category strength. slots into meals very easily because practical versatility matters a lot for dairy.

now the downsides. very dense and easy to overshoot because the nutrient density is real, but so is the concentration. fat quality varies wildly across dairy because some dairy is clean, some really is not. processing changes the category a lot because the label alone does not tell the whole story.
B tier. Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
